name: Honolulu (United States)

colors.symbolism: Hawaiian royalty and the aliʻi feather cloaks

description: A plain yellow field bearing the black coat of arms of the City and County of Honolulu at the centre.
history: Honolulu's flag uses the yellow associated with Hawaiian royalty as its field, charged with the city-and-county coat of arms derived from the heraldry of the former Kingdom of Hawaii.

features.symbolism: Yellow recalls the royal feather cloaks of the Hawaiian aliʻi (chiefs) and the colour traditionally associated with Hawaiian royalty.

features.role: Coat of arms of the City and County of Honolulu
features.symbolism: A heraldic shield based on the arms of the Kingdom of Hawaii — quartered with two royal banners and a heraldic puloʻuloʻu (tabu staff), supported by the chiefs Kameʻeiamoku and Kamanawa — proclaiming Honolulu's role as the Hawaiian capital.
